message:
test(conformance): differential harness for time.h vs glibc

CONFORMANCE: libc time.h diff matrix.

5 functions covered (gmtime_r, timegm, mktime, difftime, strftime)
with 11 epoch fixtures spanning:
- Unix epoch (0), epoch+/-1, epoch+/-day
- Leap-year boundaries (2000-02-29, 2020 vs 1900 century rule)
- Modern wide range (1234567890, 2030, 9999)
- Pre-epoch values (-86400, 1900-01-01)

Per epoch we compare:
- gmtime_r → exact tm_struct equality (sec/min/hour/mday/mon/year/wday/yday/dst)
- timegm  → epoch round-trip
- mktime  → epoch under TZ=UTC0 (TZ pinned in test, prior value restored)
- difftime → IEEE-754 bit equality across 7 (a,b) pairs incl. boundaries
- strftime → exact byte output across 15 format strings (ISO date/time,
  abbrev/full weekday/month, day-of-year, week-of-year Sun/Mon, AM/PM,
  12h clock, %% literal, plain text)

165 strftime calls (11 epochs × 15 formats), 33 calendar-conversion
calls, 7 difftime calls = 205 differential calls total.
0 divergences observed; time.h family is conformant in UTC.

TZ_LOCK Mutex serializes the test crate's TZ env mutations; prior
TZ value is restored after every with_utc() block.

Out of scope: localtime/strptime under non-UTC zones (DST handling
is implementation-specific and TZ-database-version-dependent;
deferred to a separate harness if needed).

Full abi suite: 3016/3016 pass across 62 suites (was 3010/61;
+6 from new harness).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
